SRX.Metatype.grantCloseCombat: Set Close Combat to starting rank {rating} (p. 12)
SRX.Metatype.grantLifestyle: Set lifestyle to {lifestyle} (p. 12). If you stay at Streets, you also gain the Livin' on the Streets trait (p. 74) — add it from the Traits tab.
SRX.Metatype.choicePendingWarning: Metatype +1/−1 attribute choice not yet assigned (p. 12)
SRX.Roll.autoFail: Automatic failure — dice pool reduced to 0 or less (p. 9).
SRX.Roll.noBuyHitsLiability: Hits cannot be bought under Liability (p. 10) — rolling instead.
SRX.Combat.defensePrefilled: Pre-filled from combat state and the canvas — adjust if wrong.
SRX.Edge.closeCallHint: Spend 1 Edge: +2 Defense Score against one attack (p. 17).
SRX.Edge.closeCallApplied: {name} spends Edge for Close Call (+{bonus} Defense Score).
SRX.Edge.hustleHint: Spend 1 Edge: change one Initiative die to 6 (p. 17).
SRX.Edge.secondChanceHint: Spend 1 Edge: reroll Crit Dice or non-Crit dice (p. 17).
SRX.Edge.secondChanceApplied: {name} spends Edge for Second Chance ({which}) — {hits} hits.
SRX.Edge.alreadySpent: Edge was already spent on this test.
SRX.Edge.noActor: No actor found for this roll.
SRX.Edge.notOwner: You do not own this actor.
SRX.Edge.noRoll: No evaluated roll on this message.
SRX.Edge.nothingToReroll: Nothing to reroll in that group.
SRX.Import.title: Import SRX Catalog
SRX.Import.menuHint: Import SRX Character Builder catalog files (.txt.deploy) into world items (weapons, armor, gear, talents, spells, ware, vehicles, and more).
SRX.Import.instructions: Select one or more .txt.deploy files from your SRX Character Builder "Load Data" folder. Known catalogs include Weapons, Armor, Gear, Talents, Spells, Ware, Vehicles, Anima, Traits, Contacts, Knowledge, and related tables. No rules text is bundled — you must own the free builder data.
SRX.Import.gmOnly: Only the GM can import catalog content.
SRX.Import.jsonActors: JSON files may be pregen character exports or threat catalogs (in addition to .txt.deploy catalog files).
SRX.Item.summaryHint: One line — shown on actor lists and chat cards
SRX.Sheet.name: Name
SRX.Sheet.rollAltAttribute: Roll with the alternate linked attribute
SRX.Sheet.modsOverride: Manual overrides (until Active Effects own these)
SRX.Sheet.modeToPlay: Switch to Play mode (cockpit — roll, cast, equip)
SRX.Sheet.modeToBuild: Switch to Build mode (edit bases, ratings, chargen)
SRX.Threat.attacksHint: Roll listed attacks against a targeted token.
SRX.Combat.notDefender: Only the defender (or GM) can resist this attack.
SRX.Combat.noResolved: No damage resolution data on this card.
SRX.Combat.passHint: End of pass: scores −10. After 4 passes, new Combat Turn.
SRX.Combat.actionMajorHint: Major Action — one per Action Phase; exclusive with a Complex Action.
SRX.Combat.actionMinorHint: Minor Actions — up to 2 per phase, or 1 if you also take a Major.
SRX.Combat.actionComplexHint: Complex Action — replaces your Major and Minor Actions this phase.
SRX.Combat.fullDefenseHint: Spend your Major Action: +2 Defense Score until your next Action Phase (p. 116).
SRX.Combat.fullDefenseApplied: {name} takes Full Defense (+2 Defense Score until next Action Phase).
SRX.Combat.thresholdHint: Base Defense Score before cover/Full Defense. Cover and Full Defense are applied on top.
SRX.Combat.dyingStabilized: {name} stabilizes ({hits} hits vs threshold {threshold}) — Dying removed.
SRX.Combat.dyingFailed: {name} fails dying resistance ({hits} vs {threshold}) — +1 unresisted Physical.
SRX.Combat.notOwner: Only the owner (or GM) can do that.
SRX.Combat.autoOffManual: Damage automation is off — apply manually to {name}: {summary}
SRX.Aoe.notAoe: That attack mode is not an AOE.
SRX.Aoe.noCanvas: No active canvas — open a scene first.
SRX.Aoe.pickCenter: Click the canvas to choose the blast aim point.
SRX.Aoe.directHit: Direct hit — detonates on target point.
SRX.Aoe.needToken: Place and control a token for cone attacks.
SRX.Aoe.coneConfirm: Fire cone out to {range}m (DV {dv}{type})? Tokens in the cone are auto-hit.
SRX.Aoe.regionFailed: Could not place the AOE template (see console); target resolution continues.
SRX.Aoe.scatterOff: Scatter automation is off — detonating at the aimed point; adjudicate deviation manually.
SRX.Aoe.resistOptionsHint: Good Cover grants Leverage on the resistance test. Confined spaces impose Liability.
SRX.Healing.StabilizedSuccess: {healer} stabilizes {target} ({hits} hits vs threshold {threshold}) — Dying removed.
SRX.Healing.StabilizedFailure: {healer} fails to stabilize {target} ({hits} hits vs threshold {threshold}).
SRX.Healing.FirstAidSuccess: {healer} performs First Aid on {target}, healing {boxes} boxes.
SRX.Healing.FirstAidFailure: {healer} fails to perform First Aid on {target}.
SRX.Healing.TargetDying: Select a dying target to stabilize.
SRX.Healing.SelectHealerWarning: Select a healer token (or assign a character) first.
SRX.Suppress.title: Suppressive Fire
SRX.Suppress.started: {name} lays down suppressing fire (DV {dv} AOE, {width}×{depth} m) until their next Action Phase.
SRX.Suppress.hit: {name} is caught in suppressing fire (DV {dv}) — no cover.
SRX.Settings.autoHint: How much the system automates this subsystem for the GM.
SRX.Settings.autoDamageHint: Off: monitors are never written — cards report what to apply by hand. Prompt: Apply Damage stays a button on chat cards. Automatic: damage applies as soon as resistance resolves.
SRX.Settings.autoStatusTicksHint: Off: no automated dying tests, acid/fire ticks, or shake-off prompts at phase boundaries. Prompt and Automatic both run them — the chat cards carry the follow-up buttons.
SRX.Settings.autoScatterHint: Off: no scatter roll — blasts detonate at the aimed point and you adjudicate deviation. Prompt and Automatic roll scatter and offset the blast.
SRX.Settings.autoToxinHint: Off: no world-time scheduling of toxin onsets, retests, or expiry. Prompt and Automatic schedule and fire them as world time advances.
SRX.Settings.autoSuppressHint: Off: no automatic checks when a phase starts inside a suppression zone. Prompt and Automatic post the resistance prompt for caught tokens.
SRX.Magic.notSpell: That is not a spell item.
SRX.Magic.selectTargets: Select target(s) before casting a non-self spell.
SRX.Conjure.priorReleased: Previous spirit ({name}) released — one spirit at a time.
SRX.Magic.drainResult: {name}: Drain hits {hits} vs base {base} → takes {taken} Drain.
SRX.Magic.noTargets: No targets — resolved on self / empty.
SRX.Magic.sustainHint: Sustaining {count} effect(s): {pen} dice applied automatically (Drain included). Dice Mod is for situational modifiers only.
SRX.Magic.needActor: Select a token or assign a character.
SRX.Rest.title: Rest
SRX.Qi.used: {name} uses Qi power at Force {force}; Drain hits {hits} → takes {taken}.
SRX.Conjure.needGm: GM must be online to create anima actors.
SRX.Conjure.failedUnconscious: Summoning fails — conjurer unconscious from Drain.
SRX.Conjure.drainStun: Drain: Force (Stun) — resisted with Magic + Conjuring.
SRX.Conjure.drainPhysical: Drain: Force (Physical) — resisted with Magic + Conjuring.
SRX.Astral.projectionOn: {name} projects onto the astral (budget {hours}h; used {usedMin} min).
SRX.Astral.budgetExhausted: Projection budget exhausted until a full rest.
SRX.Qi.genericEffect: Apply the power's effect narratively (Leverage, Liability, etc.).
SRX.Socket.noGm: No active GM to apply this action.
SRX.Matrix.osHint: Counts your failed Hacking tests — one global counter. Resets to 0 on Disconnect (p. 150).
SRX.Matrix.hotSimHint: Requires a cyberdeck + DNI, chosen at connection. Hacking without it suffers Liability; with it you can take biofeedback damage (p. 142).
SRX.Matrix.connected: {name} connects ({mode}{hotSim}) — online at the end of the next Combat Turn (p. 144).
SRX.Matrix.disconnected: {name} disconnects — Overwatch Score resets, persona icon vanishes.
SRX.Matrix.linkLocked: Link-locked — cannot Disconnect or Switch Interface (p. 145).
SRX.Matrix.silentOn: {name} runs silent — persona and owned icons suppressed.
SRX.Matrix.matrixDefenseApplied: {name} braces their firewall (+1 Matrix Defense, 1 free hit on firewall tests until next Action Phase).
SRX.Matrix.notHotSimFact: Not in hot-sim — Liability on Hacking tests (p. 142).
SRX.Matrix.biofeedbackImmune: Not in hot-sim — immune to this biofeedback damage (p. 148).
SRX.Matrix.noTarget: No target — enter the defender's Matrix Defense Score as the threshold.
SRX.Host.firewallHint: Logic + Software firewall pool = Host Rating × 3 (p. 151). Used for Ending-a-Program contests.
SRX.Ic.acidHint: Dumps you with dumpshock; bad failures brick the connecting device.
SRX.Ic.alertHint: Ramps up security on critical systems and files.
SRX.Ic.blackHint: Physical biofeedback damage, scaling with your OS.
SRX.Ic.blasterHint: Stun biofeedback damage (does not scale with OS).
SRX.Ic.bouncerHint: Dumps you from the Matrix — Disconnected status + dumpshock.
SRX.Ic.crashHint: Crashes one (or all) of your administered programs.
SRX.Ic.greyHint: Stun biofeedback damage, scaling with your OS.
SRX.Ic.killerHint: Penalty on your biofeedback resistance tests until you disconnect.
SRX.Ic.notifyHint: Spots you and summons a metahuman spider.
SRX.Ic.patrolHint: Penalty on all your Hacking tests equal to your OS, until you disconnect.
SRX.Ic.securityHint: Forces a Logic + Hacking test at the end of every Combat Turn or IC triggers again.
SRX.Ic.tarBabyHint: Link-locks you — no Disconnect or interface switch.
SRX.Ic.traceHint: The host learns your exact physical location.
SRX.Vehicle.noOperator: No operator — use Take Controls, or switch to Autopilot.
SRX.Vehicle.remoteNoiseFact: Remote operation — noise applies to all tests (p. 194).
SRX.Vehicle.woundedFact: Vehicle is Wounded — −1 hit already applied (stacks with operator Wounded).
SRX.Vehicle.ramHit: {vehicle} rams {target}: {dv}P to the target; {self}P back to the rammer (target's Body).
SRX.Vehicle.ramMiss: {vehicle} fails to ram {target} ({hits} hits vs DS {ds}).
SRX.Vehicle.ramSlowNote: If the target is a pedestrian, stationary, or slow: add the rammer's Speed ({speed}) to the DV (p. 200).
SRX.Vehicle.resistNote: Vehicles resist Stun and Cold with Leverage, AOE with Liability (p. 195).
SRX.Vehicle.crashResult: {name} crashes — {dv}P (Speed × 5{light}). Passengers each take half of the damage the vehicle takes (after resistance) as Physical, and any passenger who takes damage is Dazed (p. 198).
SRX.Vehicle.envHint: Roll at the start of the chase test (end of every Combat Turn). Speed environment: add Speed to step-2 hits (p. 202).
SRX.Status.blindedHint: Cannot see; many tests suffer Can't See Target.
SRX.Status.disconnectedHint: Cut off from the Matrix / wireless.
SRX.Status.dyingHint: At or past Physical Health; death tests apply.
SRX.Status.grabbedHint: In a subdual; escape threshold applies.
SRX.Status.woundedHint: −1 hit on tests (except resistance); −1 Defense Score.
SRX.Matrix.maintenancePenalty: Maintenance penalty: {n} dice to all other tests.
SRX.Matrix.livingLockout: Living Persona locked out for {n} more hour(s).
SRX.Matrix.livingBrickResult: {physical} Physical damage; cannot reconnect for {hours} hour(s).
SRX.Matrix.noDevices: No devices in this persona's network.
SRX.Matrix.importDone: Imported Matrix talents: {hacking} Hacking, {software} Software, {threading} Threading.
SRX.Vehicle.mountsNone: No weapon mounts installed. Switch to Build to add one.
SRX.Vehicle.mountFired: This weapon has already fired this Initiative Pass (p. 199).
SRX.Vehicle.mountFacing: {mount} can't bear on a target in that direction.
SRX.Vehicle.mountNeedTarget: Target a token before firing a mount.
SRX.Vehicle.dccInitResult: DCC rolls Initiative {score}; all assigned drones act on it: {drones}.
SRX.Vehicle.dccInitNote: You may lower the DCC's Initiative to your own so the drones act right after you (p. 196).
SRX.Vehicle.dccAlready: That drone is already on your DCC.
SRX.Vehicle.dccFull: Your DCC is at capacity ({n} drones).
SRX.Vehicle.dccNoCombat: Start a combat before rolling DCC Initiative.
SRX.Vehicle.dccNoDrones: None of your DCC's drones are in the active combat.
SRX.Vehicle.dccNoCharacter: Assign a character to your user before using a DCC.
SRX.Vehicle.repairNone: This vehicle has no damage to repair.
SRX.Vehicle.repairDiyNote: DIY: extended Logic + Engineering test (threshold = damage ÷ 5). Each net hit waives one point's cost.
SRX.Vehicle.repairNoTest: Hired repair needs no test by the PC (p. 196).
SRX.Vehicle.repairNoSkill: No repairer with Logic + Engineering is selected.
SRX.Vehicle.repairFail: Repair failed ({hits} hits vs threshold {threshold}) — the damage is past your ability (p. 196).
SRX.Vehicle.chaseNoCombat: Chases run inside combat — start a combat first.
SRX.Vehicle.chaseNoEnv: Roll the Environment before resolving the chase turn.
SRX.Vehicle.chaseNeedRoles: Mark at least one quarry and one pursuer.
SRX.Vehicle.chaseHint: Roll the Environment, enter each driver's chase-test hits, then Resolve. Speed is added to hits only in a Speed Environment.
SRX.Foci.notBonded: A focus must be bonded before it can be activated.
SRX.Foci.overLimit: {name} has {over} focus/foci over the safe limit ({limit}): Liability on resistance & Drain tests, and 1 unresisted Stun/hour per focus over.
SRX.Healing.SystemShockThrottle: System Shock absorbs {throttled} box(es) of {name}'s healing.
SRX.Astral.budgetFatal: {name} overstays the projection budget — the astral form ceases and the body dies.
SRX.Astral.budgetLow: {name}'s projection budget is nearly spent ({minutes} min left).
